iti109:0.3.eng: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching

iti109:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti109:2.1.pli: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso nadiyā sotena ovuyheyya piyarūpasātarūpena.
iti109:2.1.eng: “Suppose a person was being carried along by a river current that seemed nice and pleasant.

iti109:2.2.pli: Tamenaṁ cakkhumā puriso tīre ṭhito disvā evaṁ vadeyya:
iti109:2.2.eng: If a person with clear eyes saw them, they’d say:

iti109:2.3.pli: ‘kiñcāpi kho tvaṁ, ambho purisa, nadiyā sotena ovuyhasi piyarūpasātarūpena, atthi cettha heṭṭhā rahado saūmi sāvaṭṭo sagaho sarakkhaso yaṁ tvaṁ, ambho purisa, rahadaṁ pāpuṇitvā maraṇaṁ vā nigacchasi maraṇamattaṁ vā dukkhan’ti.
iti109:2.3.eng: ‘Worthy man, even though the river current carrying you along seems nice and pleasant, downstream there is a lake with waves and whirlpools, saltwater crocodiles and monsters. When you reach there it will result in death or deadly pain.’

iti109:2.4.pli: Atha kho so, bhikkhave, puriso tassa purisassa saddaṁ sutvā hatthehi ca pādehi ca paṭisotaṁ vāyameyya.
iti109:2.4.eng: Then, when they heard what was said, they’d paddle against the stream using their hands and feet.

iti109:3.1.pli: Upamā kho me ayaṁ, bhikkhave, katā atthassa viññāpanāya.
iti109:3.1.eng: I’ve made up this simile to make a point.

iti109:3.3.eng: ‘River current’ is a term for craving.

iti109:4.1.pli: ‘Piyarūpaṁ sātarūpan’ti kho, bhikkhave, channetaṁ ajjhattikānaṁ āyatanānaṁ adhivacanaṁ.
iti109:4.1.eng: ‘Seeming nice and pleasant’ is a term for the six interior sense fields.

iti109:5.1.pli: ‘Heṭṭhā rahado’ti kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ adhivacanaṁ.
iti109:5.1.eng: ‘A downstream lake’ is a term for the five lower fetters.

iti109:6.1.eng: ‘Danger of waves’ is a term for anger and distress.

iti109:7.1.eng: ‘Whirlpool’ is a term for the five kinds of sensual stimulation.

iti109:8.1.eng: ‘Saltwater crocodiles and monsters’ is a term for ladies.

iti109:9.1.eng: ‘Against the stream’ is a term for renunciation.

iti109:10.1.pli: ‘Hatthehi ca pādehi ca vāyāmo’ti kho, bhikkhave, vīriyārambhassetaṁ adhivacanaṁ.
iti109:10.1.eng: ‘Paddling with hands and feet’ is a term for rousing energy.

iti109:11.1.pli: ‘Cakkhumā puriso tīre ṭhito’ti kho, bhikkhave, tathāgatassetaṁ adhivacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassā”ti.
iti109:11.1.eng: ‘A person with clear eyes’ is a term for the Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.”

iti109:12.1.eng: “In pain they’d give up sensual pleasures,

iti109:12.2.eng: aspiring to the future sanctuary from the yoke.

iti109:12.3.eng: With deep understanding and heart well freed,

iti109:12.4.comm: The duplicated tattha tattha is not used in a limiting sense such as the English “here and there”, but in a distributive sense, “in each and every place”.

iti109:12.5.eng: That knowledge master <j>who has completed the spiritual journey,

iti109:12.6.eng: and gone to the end of the world, <j>is called ‘one who has gone beyond’.”

iti109:13.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti109:13.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti11:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti11:2.1.pli: “Mohaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
iti11:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding delusion, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.

iti11:2.2.pli: Mohañca kho, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.
iti11:2.2.eng: By directly knowing and completely understanding delusion, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.”

iti11:3.2.eng: beings go to a bad place.

iti11:3.6.eng: they never return to this world.”

iti11:4.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti11:4.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti110:0.3.eng: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching

iti110:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti110:2.1.pli: “Carato cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
iti110:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while walking.

iti110:2.2.pli: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.
iti110:2.2.eng: They tolerate it and don’t give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it. Such a mendicant is said to be ‘not keen or prudent, always lazy, and lacking energy’ when walking.

iti110:2.3.pli: Carampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyoti vuccati.

iti110:3.1.pli: Ṭhitassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
iti110:3.1.eng: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while standing …

iti110:3.2.pli: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.

iti110:3.3.pli: Ṭhitopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyoti vuccati.

iti110:4.1.pli: Nisinnassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.

iti110:4.2.pli: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.

iti110:4.3.pli: Nisinnopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyoti vuccati.

iti110:5.1.pli: Sayānassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jāgarassa uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
iti110:5.1.eng: or when lying down while awake.

iti110:5.2.pli: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.
iti110:5.2.eng: They tolerate it and don’t give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.

iti110:5.3.pli: Sayānopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jāgaro evaṁbhūto anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyoti vuccati.
iti110:5.3.eng: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘not keen or prudent, always lazy, and lacking energy’ when lying down while awake.

iti110:6.1.pli: Carato cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
iti110:6.1.eng: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while walking.

iti110:6.2.pli: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
iti110:6.2.eng: They don’t tolerate them, but give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them.

iti110:6.3.pli: Carampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.
iti110:6.3.eng: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when walking.

iti110:7.1.pli: Ṭhitassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
iti110:7.1.eng: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while standing …

iti110:7.2.pli: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.

iti110:7.3.pli: Ṭhitopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.

iti110:8.1.pli: Nisinnassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.

iti110:8.2.pli: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.

iti110:8.3.pli: Nisinnopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.

iti110:9.1.pli: Sayānassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jāgarassa uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
iti110:9.1.eng: or when lying down while awake.

iti110:9.2.pli: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
iti110:9.2.eng: They don’t tolerate it, but give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.

iti110:9.3.pli: Sayānopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jāgaro evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccatī”ti.
iti110:9.3.eng: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when lying down while awake.”

iti110:10.3.eng: one who thinks a bad thought

iti110:10.4.eng: to do with the domestic life

iti110:12.1.pli: Yo ca caraṁ vā tiṭṭhaṁ vā,
iti110:12.1.eng: But one who, whether standing or walking,

iti110:13.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti110:13.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti111:0.3.eng: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching

iti111:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti111:2.1.pli: “Sampannasīlā, bhikkhave, viharatha sampannapātimokkhā; pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvutā viharatha ācāragocarasampannā aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvino; samādāya sikkhatha sikkhāpadesu.
iti111:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, live by the ethical precepts and the monastic code. Live restrained in the monastic code, conducting yourselves well and resorting for alms in suitable places. Seeing danger in the slightest fault, keep the rules you’ve undertaken.

iti111:3.1.pli: Sampannasīlānaṁ vo, bhikkhave, viharataṁ sampannapātimokkhānaṁ pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvutānaṁ viharataṁ ācāragocarasampannānaṁ aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvīnaṁ samādāya sikkhataṁ sikkhāpadesu, kimassa uttari karaṇīyaṁ?
iti111:3.1.eng: When you’ve done this, what more is there to do?

iti111:4.1.pli: Carato cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno abhijjhā, byāpādo vigato hoti, thinamiddhaṁ vigataṁ hoti, uddhaccakukkuccaṁ vigataṁ hoti, vicikicchā pahīnā hoti, āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
iti111:4.1.eng: Suppose a mendicant has got rid of desire and ill will while walking, and has given up dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt. Their energy is roused up and unflagging, their mindfulness is established and lucid, their body is tranquil and undisturbed, and their mind is immersed in samādhi and unified.

iti111:4.2.pli: Carampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.
iti111:4.2.eng: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when walking.

iti111:5.1.pli: Ṭhitassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno abhijjhā byāpādo vigato hoti …pe… thinamiddhaṁ … uddhaccakukkuccaṁ … vicikicchā pahīnā hoti, āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
iti111:5.1.eng: Suppose a mendicant has got rid of desire and ill will while standing …
iti111:5.1.comm: The pe in Mahāsaṅgīti here and in similar phrases below appears to be an error, as there is no abbreviation at this point.

iti111:5.2.pli: Ṭhitopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.

iti111:6.1.pli: Nisinnassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno abhijjhā byāpādo vigato hoti …pe… thinamiddhaṁ … uddhaccakukkuccaṁ … vicikicchā pahīnā hoti, āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.

iti111:6.2.pli: Nisinnopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.

iti111:7.1.pli: Sayānassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jāgarassa abhijjhā byāpādo vigato hoti …pe… thinamiddhaṁ … uddhaccakukkuccaṁ … vicikicchā pahīnā hoti, āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
iti111:7.1.eng: or when lying down while awake.

iti111:7.2.pli: Sayānopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jāgaro evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccatī”ti.
iti111:7.2.eng: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when lying down while awake.”

iti111:8.3.eng: a mendicant carefully bends their limbs,

iti111:9.2.eng: as far as the planet extends;

iti111:9.3.eng: they scrutinize the rise and fall

iti111:9.4.eng: of phenomena such as the aggregates.

iti111:10.3.eng: training in what leads to serenity of heart,

iti111:10.5.eng: they call that sort of mendicant

iti111:11.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti111:11.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti112:0.3.eng: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching

iti112:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti112:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, the world has been understood by the Realized One;

iti112:2.2.eng: and he is detached from the world.

iti112:2.3.eng: The origin of the world has been understood by the Realized One;

iti112:2.4.eng: and he has given up the origin of the world.

iti112:2.5.eng: The cessation of the world has been understood by the Realized One;

iti112:2.6.eng: and he has realized the cessation of the world.

iti112:2.7.eng: The practice that leads to the cessation of the world has been understood by the Realized One;

iti112:2.8.eng: and he has developed the practice that leads to the cessation of the world.

iti112:3.1.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevakassa lokassa samārakassa sabrahmakassa sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā yasmā taṁ tathāgatena abhisambuddhaṁ,
iti112:3.1.eng: In this world—with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans—whatever is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, and explored by the mind, all that has been understood by the Realized One.

iti112:3.2.eng: That’s why he’s called the ‘Realized One’.

iti112:4.1.pli: Yañca, bhikkhave, rattiṁ tathāgato anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambujjhati, yañca rattiṁ anupādisesāya nibbānadhātuyā parinibbāyati, yaṁ etasmiṁ antare bhāsati lapati niddisati, sabbaṁ taṁ tatheva hoti no aññathā,
iti112:4.1.eng: From the night when the Realized One awakens to the supreme perfect awakening until the night he becomes fully extinguished—in the element of extinguishment with no residue—everything he speaks, says, and expresses is real, not otherwise.

iti112:4.2.eng: That’s why he’s called the ‘Realized One’.

iti112:5.1.pli: Yathāvādī, bhikkhave, tathāgato tathākārī, yathākārī tathāvādī,
iti112:5.1.eng: The Realized One does as he says, and says as he does.

iti112:5.3.eng: that’s why he’s called the ‘Realized One’.

iti112:6.1.pli: Sadevake, bhikkhave, loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya tathāgato abhibhū anabhibhūto aññadatthudaso vasavattī,
iti112:6.1.eng: In this world—with its gods, Māras, and divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, gods and humans—the Realized One is the vanquisher, the unvanquished, the universal seer, the wielder of power.

iti112:6.2.eng: That’s why he’s called the ‘Realized One’.”

iti112:7.1.eng: “Directly knowing the whole world as it is,

iti112:7.3.eng: he is detached from the whole world,

iti112:8.1.eng: That attentive one is the champion

iti112:8.2.eng: who is released from all ties.

iti112:8.4.eng: extinguishment, fearing nothing from any quarter.

iti112:9.1.eng: He is the Buddha, with defilements ended,

iti112:9.3.eng: He has attained the end of all deeds,

iti112:9.4.eng: freed with the ending of attachments.

iti112:10.1.eng: That Blessed One is the Buddha,

iti112:10.3.eng: in all the world with its gods,

iti112:11.1.eng: And so those gods and humans,

iti112:11.2.eng: who have gone to the Buddha for refuge,

iti112:12.1.eng: ‘Tamed, he is the best of tamers,

iti112:12.2.eng: peaceful, he is the seer among the peaceful,

iti112:12.3.eng: liberated, he is the foremost of liberators,

iti112:12.4.eng: crossed over, he is the most excellent of guides across.’

iti112:13.3.eng: In the world with its gods,

iti112:14.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti112:14.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti112:17.5.eng: The So it Was Said is finished.

iti12:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti12:2.1.pli: “Kodhaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
iti12:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding anger, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.

iti12:2.2.pli: Kodhañca kho, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.
iti12:2.2.eng: By directly knowing and completely understanding anger, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.

iti12:3.2.eng: beings go to a bad place.

iti12:3.6.eng: they never return to this world.”

iti12:4.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti12:4.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti13:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti13:2.1.pli: “Makkhaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
iti13:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding disdain, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.

iti13:2.2.pli: Makkhañca kho, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.
iti13:2.2.eng: By directly knowing and completely understanding disdain, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.”

iti13:3.2.eng: beings go to a bad place.

iti13:3.6.eng: they never return to this world.”

iti13:4.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti13:4.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti14:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti14:2.1.pli: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekanīvaraṇampi samanupassāmi yena nīvaraṇena nivutā pajā dīgharattaṁ sandhāvanti saṁsaranti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, avijjānīvaraṇaṁ.
iti14:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, I do not see a single shroud, shrouded by which people wander and transmigrate for a long time like the shroud of ignorance.

iti14:2.2.pli: Avijjānīvaraṇena hi, bhikkhave, nivutā pajā dīgharattaṁ sandhāvanti saṁsarantī”ti.
iti14:2.2.eng: Shrouded by ignorance, people wander and transmigrate for a long time.”

iti14:4.1.eng: Those who have given up delusion,

iti14:4.4.eng: the root is not found in them.”

iti14:5.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti14:5.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti15:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti15:2.1.pli: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekasaṁyojanampi samanupassāmi yena saṁyojanena saṁyuttā sattā dīgharattaṁ sandhāvanti saṁsaranti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, taṇhāsaṁyojanaṁ.
iti15:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, I do not see a single fetter, fettered by which people wander and transmigrate for a long time like the fetter of craving.

iti15:2.2.pli: Taṇhāsaṁyojanena hi, bhikkhave, saṁyuttā sattā dīgharattaṁ sandhāvanti saṁsarantī”ti.
iti15:2.2.eng: Fettered by craving, people wander and transmigrate for a long time.”

iti15:3.2.eng: as they transmigrate on this long journey.

iti15:3.3.eng: They go from this state to another,

iti15:4.2.eng: that craving is the cause of suffering—

iti15:4.3.eng: rid of craving, free of grasping,

iti15:5.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti15:5.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

iti16:1.1.eng: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

iti16:2.1.pli: “Sekhassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno appattamānasassa anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ patthayamānassa viharato ajjhattikaṁ aṅganti karitvā nāññaṁ ekaṅgampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ bahūpakāraṁ yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, yoniso manasikāro.
iti16:2.1.eng: “Taking into account interior factors, mendicants, I do not see a single one that is so very helpful as rational application of mind for a trainee mendicant who hasn’t achieved their heart’s desire, but lives aspiring to the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.

iti16:2.2.pli: Yoniso, bhikkhave, bhikkhu manasi karonto akusalaṁ pajahati, kusalaṁ bhāvetī”ti.
iti16:2.2.eng: A mendicant rationally applying the mind gives up the unskillful and develops the skillful.”

iti16:4.1.pli: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.
iti16:4.1.eng: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
